reply:
[[BLOCK:slide69_shape1]]
Empirical research confirms the superiority of Generalized Regression (GR).  
A cohort study (Kan, Kharrazi, Chang, Bodycombe, Lemke, & Weiner, 2019) analyzed data from 81,106 Medicare Advantage patients who had continuous medical and pharmacy insurance coverage over a five-year period (2009–2013). Total health care costs in 2013 were predicted using comorbidity indicators from 2009 to 2012.  
When utilizing 2012 data, OLS Regression performed poorly (e.g., R Square = 16.3%) compared to penalized linear regression models, which achieved R Square values ranging from 16.8% to 16.9%.  
When predictors from 2009–2012 were employed, the performance gap widened further, with R Square values of 15.0% for OLS Regression versus 18.0% to 18.2% for penalized models.
